section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's hemoglobin a1c is over the past couple months.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The intent appears to be an objective lab result (elevated A1c over past months), which belongs in O, but the sentence is incomplete/unclear. This is more a clarity issue than a section misplacement.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient will continue on albuterol, two puffs every four to six hours as needed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a treatment plan/medication management decision (continuation of therapy), which belongs in the Plan section, not in Objective findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient will add symbicort, two puffs twice a day during the summer.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a new medication order (addition of Symbicort) and timing instructions, which are explicit management actions and should be in the Plan section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient will increase the metformin dose.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This describes a change in medication dosing, which is a treatment plan item and belongs in the Plan section.
This is a statement of current/past medication use, not a new order or change. Per instructions, current medications belong in Subjective unless framed as a treatment change.
